# Synthetic KVBM /metrics exposition representing a healthy Dynamo
# deployment with KVBM tiering active. KVBM is exposed on a SEPARATE
# Prometheus endpoint from the main Dynamo frontend — default port
# 6880 via DYN_KVBM_METRICS_PORT — and only when the operator
# launches Dynamo with DYN_KVBM_METRICS=true.
#
# Metric names are taken from the authoritative Dynamo sources, all
# cross-checked against the live Grafana dashboard that queries this
# endpoint:
#   lib/runtime/src/metrics/prometheus_names.rs (bare constants)
#   deploy/observability/grafana_dashboards/kvbm.json (runtime PromQL)
#   deploy/observability/prometheus.yml (:6880 scrape target)
#
# Note on naming: KVBM constants are emitted WITHOUT a `dynamo_`
# prefix. `kvbm_offload_blocks_d2h` is the literal final metric name,
# not a bare suffix that gets prefixed. This was confirmed by the
# kvbm.json dashboard which queries the metrics directly as
# `kvbm_*`. InferScope has always had these names correct.
#
# The three KVBM metrics that exist in prometheus_names.rs but are NOT
# charted in kvbm.json (offload_bytes_object, onboard_bytes_object,
# object_read_failures, object_write_failures) are omitted here —
# they're optional tier-3 object-storage telemetry that only appears
# when the operator configures an S3/GCS tier. A KVBM deployment with
# only GPU + CPU + SSD tiers won't emit them, and the canary test
# doesn't enforce their presence.
#
# Values represent a healthy tiered cache: GPU tier is the primary
# hit path (~75% host tier hit rate means the CPU tier is serving as
# effective spillover for requests that didn't fit on GPU), with a
# small but nonzero disk tier hit rate for very long sessions.

# HELP kvbm_offload_blocks_d2h GPU-to-host KV block offload operations
# TYPE kvbm_offload_blocks_d2h counter
kvbm_offload_blocks_d2h 12500

# HELP kvbm_offload_blocks_h2d Host-to-disk KV block offload operations
# TYPE kvbm_offload_blocks_h2d counter
kvbm_offload_blocks_h2d 3200

# HELP kvbm_offload_blocks_d2d Device-to-device KV block offload operations
# TYPE kvbm_offload_blocks_d2d counter
kvbm_offload_blocks_d2d 850

# HELP kvbm_onboard_blocks_h2d Host-to-device KV block onboard operations
# TYPE kvbm_onboard_blocks_h2d counter
kvbm_onboard_blocks_h2d 9400

# HELP kvbm_onboard_blocks_d2d Device-to-device KV block onboard operations
# TYPE kvbm_onboard_blocks_d2d counter
kvbm_onboard_blocks_d2d 820

# HELP kvbm_matched_tokens Tokens reused from KVBM cache
# TYPE kvbm_matched_tokens counter
kvbm_matched_tokens 88000000

# HELP kvbm_host_cache_hit_rate CPU tier KV cache hit rate
# TYPE kvbm_host_cache_hit_rate gauge
kvbm_host_cache_hit_rate 0.75

# HELP kvbm_disk_cache_hit_rate Disk tier KV cache hit rate
# TYPE kvbm_disk_cache_hit_rate gauge
kvbm_disk_cache_hit_rate 0.18

# HELP kvbm_object_cache_hit_rate Object storage tier KV cache hit rate
# TYPE kvbm_object_cache_hit_rate gauge
kvbm_object_cache_hit_rate 0.0
